description:
## Question

How is the superwhisper on/off indicator added to this repo's existing statusline?

The repo's statusline is a pkgs.writeShellApplication in home-modules/settings/programs/claude-code.nix (jq over the Claude Code JSON blob). The reference statusline appends a ✓/✗ superwhisper segment by reading the per-project /tmp flag. Design:
- Merge the indicator into the existing statusline script (must read the same flag convention settled in ticket 4), or keep it minimal.
- Gate the segment on the voice feature so non-voice Macs don't show it.

HITL prototype.

comments.text:
RESOLVED (prototype/design). Blueprint: docs/research/voice-statusline-design.md (committed nix-4t3.5). Real module lands in nix-4t3.6.

SEAM: statusline is one indivisible writeShellApplication -> model it as COMPOSABLE SEGMENTS so voice owns its segment without owning the whole script. Revises the nix-4t3.2 'voice owns statusline' note to: 'statusline is a shared segmented surface; voice owns its segment.'

SEGMENT MODEL: submodule { priority:int; text:lines (reads $input JSON, echoes segment or nothing); runtimeInputs:listOf package }. Generator reads JSON once, runs segments in priority order, drops empties, joins with ' · '. runtimeInputs aggregated.

PLACEMENT:
- NEW module home-modules/claude-statusline.nix — own namespace programs.claudeStatusline.{enable,segments} (NOT under upstream programs.claude-code, which rejects undeclared options). Builds generator, sets programs.claude-code.settings.statusLine.command gated on claude-code.enable.
- claude-code.nix: drop the statusline let-binding + statusLine setting; the model+ctx content becomes the BASE segment (priority=10, runtimeInputs=[jq]).
- voice.nix: user.programs.claudeStatusline.segments += SW segment (priority=90) under mkIf voice.enable.

GATING: SW segment is contributed by voice.nix (darwin) under voice.enable, so it is ABSENT on shirka (linux+firefly, runs claude-code, no cask) and non-voice Macs — module gate does the work, no cross-scope nix ref. Lightweight '-x' binary guard kept only to avoid a false ✓ before first cask install.

CORRECTNESS: SW segment hashes .workspace.current_dir (the session cwd) — the exact dir the hook binary + /superwhisper skill (nix-4t3.4) hash — so it tracks /tmp/superwhisper-agent/disabled-<md5(cwd)>. (Not the statusline process $PWD, which may differ.)

title: Design the statusline SuperWhisper on/off indicator
